name: I got hurt on a machine at work yesterday, what do I do right now?
description: Report to employer immediately, get medical help, document details—delays can deny claims[4].

mainEntity.name: I got hurt on a machine at work yesterday, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to employer immediately, get medical help, document details—delays can deny claims[4].
acceptedAnswer.description: **Immediately report the injury to your employer and seek medical treatment.** In most states, notify your supervisor or HR in writing within 24-72 hours to start the workers' comp process[4]. Delaying can jeopardize your claim. **Key factors:** Document everything including time, witnesses, and machine details; get examined even if symptoms seem minor. **Practical guidance:** Avoid signing anything from insurance without advice; take photos of the scene if possible. Consult a workers' comp attorney promptly for free evaluation. Utah and Idaho require prompt reporting to preserve benefits[1][2].
